engine.goal: Fix the send-money balance UX so wallet balance is shown split by sellable asset and transfer preflight enforces single-asset sellability instead of aggregated balance.

inspect_balance_and_preflight_gap.root_cause: The repo had partial, uncommitted wallet-balance changes in scripts/unigox-client.ts that exposed split USDC/USDT balances, but the orchestrator still surfaced only total balance, still quoted only USDC, and still treated aggregate balance as if one SELL could combine assets. That left status reporting stale and could misstate sellability when total balance was enough but no single asset could fund the trade.

design_asset_specific_sellability_fix.design_notes: Keep the scope surgical: preserve the existing transfer flow, add reusable balance-formatting helpers, evaluate preflight quotes per sellable asset (USDC and USDT), pick a single coverable asset for execution, and explicitly block plus explain the aggregate-enough-but-split-insufficient case.
design_asset_specific_sellability_fix.flow_sequence: startup auth snapshot -> show total plus per-asset balances -> ensure payment details -> fetch per-asset preflight coverage -> choose one fundable asset or block -> carry selected asset into createTradeRequest -> keep top-up messaging aligned with single-asset coverage.

validate_asset_specific_sellability_fix.test_results: PASS (31/31 tests). Added regressions for split balance display at flow start, single-asset selection for execution, and aggregate-total-but-no-single-asset coverage blocking.

commit_asset_specific_sellability_fix.commit_sha: captured in git history / task summary
